codeReview.summary: The feature cleanly refactors liquidation logic into reusable internal helpers and adds atomic multi-borrower liquidation with per-position Liquidated events. The implementation matches the feature description, and the only gap I found is a non-blocking coverage hole around the new indexed failure error.

issues.description: The failure-path test only asserts a generic revert for a mixed healthy/unhealthy batch, so it does not verify the new contract guarantee that failures are wrapped as `BatchLiquidationFailed(index, reason)`. If the index propagation regresses later, this suite would still pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library does not capture a useful liquidation-testing quirk: bad-debt scenarios require positions to be deeply underwater, otherwise a tiny residual collateral amount can prevent the `position.collateralAmount == 0` branch and leave debt uncleared in tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The worker transcript skeleton shows the feature work stalled until this was derived from the math (`worker-transcripts.jsonl`, session `aecfda5a-f723-4b8b-ad78-581c2c37e0f5`), and the final test now documents the workaround in `dualvm/test/BatchLiquidation.ts:30-31,141`. No corresponding note exists in `.factory/library/architecture.md` or `.factory/library/environment.md`.

summary: Review passed. `LendingCore` now supports batch liquidation through shared internal liquidation helpers, and the diff covers the required multi-position flow and event emission. One non-blocking test gap remains: the suite does not assert the indexed `BatchLiquidationFailed` wrapper on failure-path batches.
